~ enough for the rs or so cents a
day more that contractors might

The movement to fill the city
with idle men Is simply prelude
to the breaking down Of all wage
scales.


An instance Of the city's caIlous
disregard Of home Iaboring inter-
ests Is shown by the action Of the
commissioners yesterday in consid-
ering bids on the new high pres
sure main on jackson to Division.
Four miles of water main are to be
laid, an unde taking that would
furnish employment for several
hundred citizens at living wages by
the cay tabor plan of doing city
work that has almost become ex-
tinct under the present administra-
ton The cheap labor invited to
Spokane will profit by this work
under the contract system by
which the main is to be laid. The
contractor will do the work on the
basis Of $2.75 per day and pay
what he. actually has to pay.


Here is what State Senator r.
A. Hutchinson has to say on the
subject:


~ have read with considerable
interest the article wherein Mayor
Moore Is said to be endeavoring to
provsse fund to employ idle men
who drift to our city during the
winter months. at rs cents per
day or some such wages. am
opposed to Such scheme for the
reason that we have thousands Of
industrious, frugal working men
right here who are the backbone Of
our city. They are engaged in pay
ing for their homes, in rearing and
educating their families, and every
dollar they earn Is spent in this
city, contributing in great measure
to the city's prosperity. The only
capital Of workingmen is their la
Dor, and the city has no right to
pauperize that labor by establish
ing a wage scale under any pre-
text that destroys this capital


eff there are a number of men
here that come in from the outside
and have to be fed as a measure
Of charity, the city should start
some kind of municipal work where
so many hours labor would entitIe
the work to plain food only. Keep
these men from starving II there is
no other way, but give them to un-
derstand that Spokane is not a
Mecca for the shiftIess and the
hobo.


- hold that the present wage
scale should be maintained for the
reason that the city paying fair
wage to its employees tends to keep
= fair wage scale for all other
workers in the city and for the
further reason that all contracts
have been let on that basis and
the property owners would not
benefit by any reduction. in wages.
Instead they would be. damaged by
the reduced wages not correspond
ing to the price Of living.


~ think we should have strict
laws prohibiting the employment Of
anyone on state or municipal work
who is not citizen, for, as stated
above, 1t Is the citizen worker that
supports the commonwealth" :.
